name: Photolyase-containing cream lowered UV-induced CPDs and apoptosis in human skin
summary: A small human UV-challenge study supports topical photolyase as a skin-local DNA-damage biomarker intervention.
statement: Topical photolyase-containing DNA repair enzyme cream reduced ultraviolet-induced cyclobutane pyrimidine dimers and apoptosis in human skin compared with sunscreen alone.
population_or_model: Human volunteers in a UV-challenge design
quantitative_note: The abstract reports superiority for CPDs and apoptosis, both P<0.001.
caveats: This was an acute UV-challenge biomarker study, not a clinical aging or cancer endpoint trial.
The effect is skin-local and cannot be assumed to translate to systemic DNA repair.
